title: An Account of Sa-Go-Ye-Wat-Ha, or Red Jacket, and His People, 1750-1830

memes.description: 'Table of contents for An Account of Sa-Go-Ye-Wat-Ha, or Red Jacket, and His People, 1750-1830'

memes.label: "Albany"
memes.description: 'Albany was the capital of New York State and the site of early treaty councils between the state government and the Iroquois — a political center where land transactions affecting the Seneca were authorized and where the policies that shaped the dispossession of the Six Nations were formulated.'

memes.label: "Albert H. Tracy"
memes.description: 'A prominent western New York public man whose eyewitness notes and recollections of Red Jacket in council, furnished to biographer William L. Stone, preserved several of the orator''s later utterances and a vivid impression of his eloquence.'

memes.label: "American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions"
memes.description: "The American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions (ABCFM), also known as the Boston Missionary Society, was the Protestant missionary organization that sent missionaries to the Seneca in the early nineteenth century and played a central role in the religious controversies that divided the Seneca Nation."

memes.label: "Revolutionary War and the Iroquois"
memes.description: 'The Revolutionary War split the Iroquois Confederacy — the first time the Six Nations had fought against each other — with most Seneca and Mohawk siding with Britain while the Oneida and Tuscarora supported the Americans, resulting in devastating consequences for the entire Confederacy.'

memes.label: "Anthony Wayne"
memes.description: 'American general who reorganized the United States army and, at the Battle of Fallen Timbers in August 1794, broke the resistance of the north-western tribes, closing his campaign with the 1795 treaty at Greenville during the frontier wars that entangled the Iroquois nations in the era of Red Jacket.'

memes.label: "Arthur St. Clair"
memes.description: 'American general and governor of the territory west of the Ohio whose army was surprised and routed by the western Indian tribes in November 1791, one of the heaviest frontier defeats of the early republic and part of the wars that entangled the Iroquois nations during the era of Red Jacket.'

memes.label: "Avon"
memes.description: 'Avon was a settlement in the Genesee Valley near important Seneca sites — a locality where Indian villages, mineral springs, and the early residents who recorded Seneca traditions intersected in the narrative of Red Jacket and his people.'

memes.label: "Battle of Fallen Timbers (1794)"
memes.description: "General Anthony Wayne's decisive victory at Fallen Timbers in August 1794 crushed the Western Confederacy's military resistance and paved the way for the Treaty of Canandaigua later that year — shifting the balance of power that framed all subsequent US-Iroquois diplomacy."

memes.label: "Big Tree"
memes.description: 'Big Tree was the site of the pivotal 1797 treaty council where Thomas Morris negotiated the largest Seneca land cession — a location in the Genesee Valley that became synonymous with the dispossession of the Seneca from their ancestral territory.'

memes.label: "Big Tree Treaty (1797)"
memes.description: 'The Big Tree Treaty (September 15, 1797) was the largest single [[seneca-nation|Seneca]] land cession — negotiated by Thomas Morris at [[big-tree|Big Tree]] in the [[genesee-river|Genesee Valley]], the treaty transferred approximately 3.5 million acres to American land companies while reserving a handful of Seneca reservations including [[buffalo-creek|Buffalo Creek]].'

memes.label: "Black Rock"
memes.description: 'Black Rock was a strategic crossing point on the Niagara River and the site of a gallant American defense during the War of 1812 — a location where Seneca loyalties were tested and the military frontier between the United States and British Canada was contested.'

memes.label: "British Indian Department"
memes.description: 'The British Indian Department was the government agency through which Great Britain managed relations with Native nations — its officers in Canada and at frontier posts sustained the British-Iroquois alliance and exerted persistent influence over Seneca affairs long after American independence.'

memes.label: "Buffalo Creek Reservation"
memes.description: 'Buffalo Creek was the principal Seneca reservation and Red Jacket''s home — the site of numerous councils, the arrival of missionaries, and the political center of Seneca life from the 1790s through Red Jacket''s final years.'

memes.label: "Buffalo Creek Council (1791)"
memes.description: 'The Buffalo Creek Council of April 1791 was a pivotal diplomatic encounter where Colonel Thomas Proctor met with the Seneca to negotiate land concessions — and Red Jacket delivered a speech that established his reputation as the foremost defender of Seneca sovereignty.'

memes.label: "Canada (British North America)"
memes.description: 'Canada — British North America — served as a refuge and alternative political center for Iroquois loyalists after the Revolutionary War, with British Indian Department officials in Canada exerting persistent influence over Seneca affairs and complicating US-Iroquois relations.'

memes.label: "Canandaigua"
memes.description: 'Canandaigua was a principal Seneca settlement and the site of the pivotal [[treaty-of-canandaigua|Treaty of Canandaigua]] in 1794 — a location of immense diplomatic importance where the [[seneca-nation|Seneca]] and United States established their post-Revolutionary relationship.'

memes.label: "Captain Pollard"
memes.description: "Captain Pollard was a Seneca chief and leader of the Christian party — an ally of Young King in the faction that opposed Red Jacket and sought to bring Christian missions and education to the Seneca Nation in the 1820s."

memes.label: "Cayuga Nation"
memes.description: 'One of the Six Nations of the Iroquois Confederacy — the Cayuga occupied territory in the Finger Lakes region between the Seneca to the west and the Onondaga to the east, and their lands were among those targeted in the post-Revolutionary land cessions.'

memes.label: "Christian Missions Among the Seneca"
memes.description: "Christian missionary activity among the Seneca Nation in the late 18th and early 19th centuries, which became a central source of cultural and political conflict — dividing the nation into Christian and Pagan factions and ultimately leading to Red Jacket's deposition as sachem."

memes.label: "Clan System"
memes.description: 'The Iroquois clan system was a matrilineal social structure in which descent, inheritance, and political identity passed through the mother''s line, with each nation divided into named clans such as Wolf, Bear, Turtle, and Beaver.'

memes.label: "Colonel Thomas Proctor"
memes.description: "Colonel Thomas Proctor was a United States Indian agent who, accompanied by Cornplanter, arrived at the Buffalo Creek council fire in 1791 to negotiate with the Seneca and other Iroquois nations on behalf of the federal government."

memes.label: "Iroquois Condolence Ceremony"
memes.description: "The condolence ceremony — 'wiping away the tears' — was the essential ritual that opened all Iroquois diplomatic proceedings, a deeply symbolic practice that cleared the grief of bereaved participants so they could deliberate at the council fire with clear minds and open hearts."

memes.label: "Cornplanter (John Abeel)"
memes.description: 'Cornplanter (circa 1732–1836) was a Seneca war chief and diplomat — a leading figure in Iroquois-American relations after the Revolution, who clashed with [[red-jacket|Red Jacket]] over land sales and accommodation with the United States.'

memes.label: "Council Fire"
memes.description: "The council fire was the central symbolic institution of Iroquois governance and diplomacy — the ceremonial gathering place where chiefs debated, treaties were negotiated, and the political life of the Confederacy was conducted."

memes.label: "Council at Tioga Point (1790)"
memes.description: 'The Council at Tioga Point (November 1790) was a diplomatic gathering between Colonel Timothy Pickering and the Iroquois Confederacy that prepared the ground for the Treaty of Canandaigua — an occasion that demonstrated Red Jacket''s growing stature as a diplomat and orator.'

memes.label: "Covenant Chain"
memes.description: 'The Covenant Chain was a metaphorical and diplomatic framework for the alliance between the Iroquois Confederacy and the British Crown, symbolizing a series of linked agreements that bound the parties in mutual obligation.'

memes.label: "De Witt Clinton"
memes.description: "Governor of New York who defended the Seneca Nation's right to retain their reservations against the pressure of land speculators in the 1820s."

memes.label: "Detroit"
memes.description: 'Detroit was a strategic British military post and trading center on the Detroit River — the westernmost of the five British posts retained after the Revolution, commanding the water route between Lake Erie and Lake Huron and exerting influence over the western Iroquois nations.'

memes.label: "Edward Braddock"
memes.description: 'British general whose army was defeated in 1755 by a force chiefly of Indians allied with the French — the campaign in which the young Cornplanter was among his people in the field.'

memes.label: "Eleazer Ripley"
memes.description: 'United States brigade commander in the 1814 Niagara campaign, present at the capture of Fort Erie and the battle of Chippewa alongside the Seneca warriors who served with the American army.'

memes.label: "Farmer's Brother"
memes.description: "Farmer's Brother was a Seneca chief and orator who was a contemporary and ally of Red Jacket — remembered for his objection to Red Jacket's unilateral extinguishing of the council fire during the 1797 Big Tree negotiations."

memes.label: "Fort Niagara"
memes.description: 'Fort Niagara was a strategic British military post at the mouth of the Niagara River — a refuge for Iroquois loyalists during the Revolutionary War and a persistent source of British influence over the [[seneca-nation|Seneca]] in the post-Revolutionary period.'

memes.label: "Fort Stanwix"
memes.description: 'Fort Stanwix was the site of the 1784 treaty council where Red Jacket first appeared before the public as a sachem — a crucial diplomatic event that established the post-Revolutionary relationship between the United States and the Iroquois Confederacy.'

memes.label: "Genesee River"
memes.description: 'The Genesee River was the central waterway of Seneca territory in western New York — its fertile valley was the heartland of Seneca settlement, the site of pivotal land negotiations, and a landscape of deep cultural and spiritual significance to the Iroquois people.'

memes.label: "George Hosmer"
memes.description: 'Resident of Avon in the Genesee country who accompanied Red Jacket to Indian councils and whose recollections became one of the documentary sources for anecdotes of the chief.'

memes.label: "George Washington"
memes.description: "George Washington (1732–1799), first President of the United States, appears in Hubbard's narrative as the architect of early federal Indian policy — the figure whose word at treaty councils was invoked as the highest guarantee of American promises to the Iroquois."

memes.label: "Great Spirit"
memes.description: 'The Great Spirit (Hawenneyu) was the supreme creator deity in traditional Iroquois religion — invoked in speeches, treaties, and ceremonies as the ultimate witness to human covenants by [[red-jacket|Red Jacket]] and other [[seneca-nation|Seneca]] leaders.'

memes.label: "Half Town"
memes.description: "Seneca chief and one of Cornplanter's associates who joined the delegation to Philadelphia after the Council of Tioga Point, pressing the federal government to secure his people's land south of Lake Erie, and who later led Seneca warriors on the Niagara frontier in the War of 1812."

memes.label: "Hendrick (Theyanoguin)"
memes.description: 'Hendrick (circa 1680–1755) was a Mohawk chief and diplomat — a key figure in the Anglo-Iroquois alliance during the first half of the 18th century, known for his participation in the Albany Congress of 1754 and his death at the Battle of Lake George.'

memes.label: "Henry Knox"
memes.description: "General Henry Knox, the first United States secretary of war, whom President Washington appointed with Colonel Timothy Pickering to conduct the federal government's conferences with the Six Nations during the treaty negotiations of the early 1790s."

memes.label: "Holland Land Company"
memes.description: "The Holland Land Company was a syndicate of Dutch investors who acquired vast tracts of Seneca land in the Genesee region through pre-emptive rights — its agents operated alongside the Ogden and New York and Genesee companies in western New York during the late eighteenth and early nineteenth centuries."

memes.label: "Horatio Jones"
memes.description: "American interpreter and Indian agent, captured and adopted by the Seneca, who became one of Red Jacket's favorite interpreters and a key intermediary between the Iroquois and the United States government."

memes.label: "Iroquois Confederacy"
memes.description: 'The Iroquois Confederacy (Haudenosaunee) was a league of five — later six — Native American nations in what is now upstate New York, forming one of the world''s oldest representative democracies.'

memes.label: "Iroquois Delegation to Washington (1792)"
memes.description: "The 1792 Iroquois delegation to Philadelphia — led by Red Jacket and Cornplanter — was a diplomatic embassy that brought Seneca leaders face to face with President Washington, Congress, and the land speculators whose activities were transforming their homeland."

memes.label: "Long House"
memes.description: 'The Long House (Ganonsyoni) was both the physical dwelling of Iroquois extended families and the central political metaphor of the Confederacy — the Five Nations were described as living together under one roof, with the Senecas as keepers of the western door.'

memes.label: "Iroquois Oratory"
memes.description: 'Iroquois oratory was a cultivated art of persuasive public speech — essential to council deliberation, treaty negotiation, and the preservation of tribal history — that drew the admiration of American and European observers alike.'

memes.label: "Iroquois Tradition"
memes.description: 'Iroquois tradition encompassed the body of customs, beliefs, ceremonies, and social practices transmitted orally across generations — the ancestral inheritance that Red Jacket and other conservative leaders fought to preserve against the pressures of assimilation and missionary conversion.'

memes.label: "Jacob Brown"
memes.description: 'American major general who commanded the United States forces on the Niagara frontier in 1814, capturing Fort Erie and winning the battle of Chippewa in the campaign in which Red Jacket''s Seneca warriors fought as American allies.'

memes.label: "Jasper Parrish"
memes.description: "American interpreter and Indian agent, captured as a child by the Seneca, who became one of Red Jacket's favorite interpreters and a respected intermediary in Iroquois–United States relations."

memes.label: "John Breckenridge"
memes.description: 'Clergyman who as a young candidate for the ministry met Red Jacket at Black Rock in 1821, and whose recorded conversation with the orator became a firsthand source for the account.'

memes.label: "John Butler"
memes.description: 'Loyalist officer who led Tory forces alongside Brant against the frontier settlements during the Revolutionary War, and whose interest in Iroquois lands is recorded in the councils of the Red Jacket era.'

memes.label: "John Greig"
memes.description: 'Canandaigua land agent for the Ogden Company who bought some of the smaller Seneca reservations along the Genesee over Red Jacket''s persistent opposition, and who served the author as a firsthand source for the orator''s later dealings.'

memes.label: "John Niles Hubbard"
memes.description: "John Niles Hubbard (1815–1897) was the author of *An Account of Sa-Go-Ye-Wat-Ha, or Red Jacket, and His People, 1750–1830* (1886) — the primary source for this wiki, whose biographical approach and 19th-century perspective frame the entire narrative of Red Jacket's life."

memes.label: "John Sullivan"
memes.description: 'American major general of the Continental Army whose 1779 expedition marched through the Genesee country of the Iroquois, driving the Senecas into retreat after the battle at Newtown and opening Western New York to later settlement during the era of Red Jacket.'

memes.label: "Joseph Brant (Thayendanegea)"
memes.description: 'Joseph Brant (1743–1807) was a Mohawk war chief and diplomat — a principal figure in the Iroquois alliance with Britain during the American Revolution, who afterward led the migration of Iroquois loyalists to the Grand River reserve in Ontario.'

memes.label: "Josiah Harmar"
memes.description: 'American general and Revolutionary War veteran who led the first federal expedition against the north-western tribes in 1790; its failure at the Wabash paved the way for the later campaigns of St. Clair and Wayne during the frontier wars of Red Jacket''s era.'

memes.label: "Lafayette (Marquis de Lafayette)"
memes.description: "French general and hero of the American Revolution whose 1825 return tour of the United States brought him face to face again with Red Jacket, whom he had first seen resisting the surrender of Iroquois lands at the Fort Stanwix treaty of 1784."

memes.label: "Lake Ontario"
memes.description: 'Lake Ontario formed the northern boundary of Iroquois territory and served as a strategic military corridor connecting British posts at Niagara, Oswego, and beyond — its waters were a highway for the movement of armies, refugees, and diplomatic missions throughout the narrative.'

memes.label: "Little Beard"
memes.description: "Little Beard was a Seneca chief active during the Revolutionary War period — known for his role in the border warfare of the New York-Pennsylvania frontier and his involvement in episodes that shaped the tense relationship between the Seneca and American settlers."

memes.label: "Little Billy"
memes.description: 'A Seneca chief of some note, frequently in Red Jacket''s company, remembered in Hubbard''s account for a sharp exchange over the charge of cowardice and for his part in Seneca councils and the War of 1812.'

memes.label: "Mary Jemison (Deh-he-wa-mis)"
memes.description: 'Mary Jemison (1743–1833) was a white woman captured and adopted by the Seneca as a child — who chose to remain with her adopted people, became a respected Seneca matron, and became known as the "White Woman of the Genesee."'

memes.label: "Mohawk Nation"
memes.description: "The Mohawk Nation was the easternmost of the Six Nations of the Iroquois Confederacy, known as the 'Keepers of the Eastern Door' — the people of Joseph Brant, Hendrick, and the nation most closely allied with the British Crown during the colonial and Revolutionary War periods."

memes.label: "Nathaniel Gorham"
memes.description: 'One of the two principals of the Phelps and Gorham Purchase, the 1788 speculation that acquired the pre-emptive right to more than two million acres of Seneca land in the Genesee country of western New York.'

memes.label: "Neutrality Policy"
memes.description: 'The Iroquois policy of neutrality between competing European powers — and later between Great Britain and the United States — was a strategic diplomatic doctrine that allowed the Confederacy to preserve its sovereignty amid colonial conflicts.'

memes.label: "New York and Genesee Land Company"
memes.description: 'The New York and Genesee Land Company was organized to acquire Seneca lands in the Genesee Valley — its agents, including Oliver Phelps and Robert Morris, orchestrated the land transactions that dispossessed the Seneca from their most valuable territory.'
aliases: New York and Genesee Land Company

memes.label: "New York Missionary Society"
memes.description: "The New York Missionary Society was one of the Protestant missionary organizations that, alongside the American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions, sent missionaries to work among the Seneca — contributing to the religious controversy that divided the Seneca Nation into pagan and Christian factions in the early nineteenth century."

memes.label: "Ogden Land Company"
memes.description: "The Ogden Land Company was a land speculation company led by Colonel Aaron Ogden that acquired pre-emptive rights to Seneca reservations in western New York and pursued their purchase through decades of persistent negotiation, ultimately succeeding in acquiring most Seneca lands by the 1830s."

memes.label: "Oliver Phelps"
memes.description: 'American land speculator who, with Nathaniel Gorham, secured the pre-emptive right to the Genesee country and in 1788 convened the Buffalo Creek council that opened the Phelps and Gorham Purchase, the land transaction that drew the Senecas of Red Jacket''s day into decades of cessions.'

memes.label: "Oneida Nation"
memes.description: 'One of the Six Nations of the Iroquois Confederacy — the Oneida occupied territory between the Mohawk and Onondaga and were, alongside the Tuscarora, the only Iroquois nation to support the American cause during the Revolutionary War.'

memes.label: "Onondaga Nation"
memes.description: "Central nation of the Iroquois Confederacy and the 'Keepers of the Council Fire' — the Onondaga hosted the Grand Council at their capital, which served as the political and spiritual center of the Six Nations."

memes.label: "Fort Oswego"
memes.description: 'Fort Oswego was one of five strategic British posts on the Great Lakes retained after the Revolutionary War — a military installation on Lake Ontario that symbolized Britain''s continued influence over Iroquois territory and a persistent source of friction with the United States.'

memes.label: "Pagan-Christian Divide"
memes.description: "The religious and political division of the Seneca Nation into Pagan and Christian factions in the early 19th century — a schism that culminated in the deposition of Red Jacket as sachem in 1827 and reflected the broader crisis of Iroquois identity under American expansion."

memes.label: "Peter B. Porter"
memes.description: "American general and statesman based at Black Rock, New York, who commanded Indian warriors during the War of 1812 and had great influence among the Seneca."

memes.label: "Philadelphia"
memes.description: 'Philadelphia was the seat of the United States government during the early republic — the destination for Seneca delegations and the place where Washington, Congress, and federal officials shaped the Indian policies that determined the fate of the Iroquois nations.'

memes.label: "Philip Schuyler"
memes.description: 'American major general of the Revolutionary War who convened the 1776 German Flats council seeking Iroquois neutrality, and later served among the New York commissioners negotiating Indian land purchases in the era of Red Jacket.'

memes.label: "Pulteney Association"
memes.description: "The Pulteney Association was a British land syndicate that acquired extensive holdings in the Genesee region of western New York — its agents were among the early white settlers in Seneca territory and participated in the transformation of the Genesee Valley through the late eighteenth and early nineteenth centuries."

memes.label: "Red Jacket (Sa-Go-Ye-Wat-Ha)"
memes.description: 'Red Jacket (circa 1750–1830) was a Seneca orator and sachem of the Wolf clan — the most famous Native American speaker of his era, known for his eloquent defense of Iroquois traditions, his opposition to Christian missions, and his leadership of the "Pagan party" within the [[seneca-nation|Seneca Nation]].'

memes.label: "Restoration of Red Jacket (1824)"
memes.description: 'The restoration of Red Jacket to his chieftainship after his deposition in 1824 was a dramatic political reversal — achieved through a combination of federal intercession in Washington and overwhelming popular support among the Seneca people.'

memes.label: "Robert Morris"
memes.description: 'The "Financier of the Revolution" who acquired the greater part of the Phelps and Gorham Purchase and, through his son Thomas, set in motion the land dealings that culminated in the cession of most remaining Seneca territory west of the Genesee.'

memes.label: "Sachem (Chieftainship)"
memes.description: 'The Iroquois system of hereditary civil chieftainship, in which sachems — nominated by clan mothers — served as peace-time leaders of the Confederacy council, distinct from war-chiefs.'

memes.label: "Seneca Nation"
memes.description: 'The Seneca Nation was the westernmost of the Six Nations of the [[iroquois-confederacy|Iroquois Confederacy]] — the "Keepers of the Western Door" — and the people to whom [[red-jacket|Red Jacket]] belonged, defending their sovereignty through decades of treaty negotiations and cultural resistance.'

memes.label: "Sir John Johnson"
memes.description: "Loyalist son of Sir William Johnson and an agent of the crown's Indian interest in Canada, who helped summon the Iroquois to the British side during the Revolution and afterward urged Joseph Brant and the Six Nations to resist the American occupation of the western posts."

memes.label: "Sir William Johnson"
memes.description: 'Sir William Johnson (1715–1774) was the British Superintendent of Indian Affairs for the northern colonies — the most influential European figure in Iroquois diplomacy before the American Revolution, whose personal relationships with Mohawk leaders (including his marriage to Molly Brant) shaped the British-Iroquois alliance.'

memes.label: "Society of Friends (Quakers)"
memes.description: "The Society of Friends (Quakers) sent deputations to Seneca councils and advocated for peaceful relations and fair treatment — their approach to the Seneca emphasized persuasion over coercion, education over proselytism, and moral witness over political maneuvering."

memes.label: "St. Clair's Defeat (1791)"
memes.description: "General Arthur St. Clair's crushing defeat by the Western Confederacy in 1791 was the worst American military disaster on the frontier — an event that emboldened Native resistance, alarmed the Washington administration, and set the stage for the diplomacy that would culminate in the Treaty of Canandaigua."

memes.label: "Sullivan-Clinton Campaign (1779)"
memes.description: 'The Sullivan-Clinton Campaign of 1779 was a scorched-earth military expedition ordered by General Washington that systematically destroyed Seneca and other Iroquois towns and crops, forcing thousands to flee to British posts and fundamentally altering the balance of power in Iroquois territory.'

memes.label: "Tecumseh"
memes.description: 'Shawnee war leader who sought to unite the western and southern tribes into a great confederacy against American expansion, whose movement drew in Red Jacket''s Senecas and ended with his death at the Battle of the Thames in 1813.'

memes.label: "The Prophet (Tenskwatawa)"
memes.description: 'Shawnee religious leader and brother of Tecumseh, whose town on the Wabash became the seat of the Great Western Confederacy and the site of the 1811 battle of Tippecanoe.'

memes.label: "Thomas Morris"
memes.description: 'Thomas Morris (1771–1849) was a United States Indian agent and land speculator who negotiated the controversial 1797 Treaty of Big Tree with the Seneca — a land cession that Red Jacket fiercely opposed and that became emblematic of the pressures facing Iroquois landholders in the early republic.'

memes.label: "Timothy Alden"
memes.description: 'President of Allegheny College whose account of a visit to Cornplanter on the Allegheny described the aged chief''s village and hospitality, furnishing the account a firsthand source.'

memes.label: "Timothy Pickering"
memes.description: 'Timothy Pickering (1745–1829) was a United States Secretary of State and Indian commissioner who negotiated the 1794 Treaty of Canandaigua with the Six Nations — a landmark agreement that established the federal trust relationship with the Iroquois and remains in force today.'

memes.label: "Tioga Point"
memes.description: 'Tioga Point was the site of an important 1790 council between the Iroquois and Colonel Timothy Pickering — a diplomatic gathering where Red Jacket''s growing influence was acknowledged and where ceremonies of condolence and peace were performed.'

memes.label: "Treaty Annuities"
memes.description: "The system of annual cash payments, goods, and services promised by the United States government to Native American tribes — including the Seneca — in exchange for land cessions, formalized through treaties and administered (often erratically) by federal Indian agents."

memes.label: "Treaty of Canandaigua (1794)"
memes.description: 'The Treaty of Canandaigua (November 11, 1794) established the post-Revolutionary peace between the United States and the Six Nations, defining boundaries, affirming Iroquois sovereignty over remaining lands, and establishing the diplomatic framework that governed US-Iroquois relations for decades.'

memes.label: "Treaty of Fort Stanwix (1784)"
memes.description: "The Treaty of Fort Stanwix (October 22, 1784) was the first treaty between the newly independent United States and the Iroquois Confederacy — the occasion of Red Jacket's first public appearance as a sachem and a punitive peace that imposed harsh land cessions on the Six Nations."

memes.label: "Trial of Red Jacket (1824)"
memes.description: 'The trial and deposition of Red Jacket in 1824 was the climactic episode of the religious controversy among the Seneca — a political and cultural confrontation in which Red Jacket was temporarily stripped of his chieftainship by Christian converts before being dramatically restored.'

memes.label: "Tuscarora Nation"
memes.description: 'The sixth and last nation to join the Iroquois Confederacy — the Tuscarora migrated from North Carolina in the early 18th century, were adopted into the Confederacy around 1722, and settled in western New York.'

memes.label: "Wampum"
memes.description: 'Wampum — belts and strings of polished shell beads — served as the primary medium of diplomatic record-keeping, treaty confirmation, and ceremonial communication among the Iroquois and neighboring nations.'

memes.label: "US Office of Indian Affairs"
memes.description: "The US Office of Indian Affairs, housed within the War Department in the early republic, was the federal agency responsible for managing relations with Native American nations — negotiating treaties, distributing annuities, and regulating trade — and the primary point of American governmental contact for the Seneca and other Iroquois nations."

memes.label: "War of 1812 on the Niagara Frontier"
memes.description: 'The War of 1812 on the Niagara Frontier placed the Seneca in an agonizing position between American and British forces — a conflict that saw Seneca warriors fighting on both sides, the burning of Buffalo and Black Rock, and the final severing of the British-Iroquois military alliance.'

memes.label: "Washington's Address to the Iroquois"
memes.description: "President George Washington's formal address to departing Iroquois delegates — a speech recorded by Hubbard that articulated the American policy of 'civilization' and assimilation while acknowledging Iroquois sovereignty — framed the diplomatic relationship for decades."

memes.label: "William Darke"
memes.description: 'American colonel of regulars at St. Clair''s defeat of 1791, ordered to charge the concealed Indians with the bayonet and later to cover the army''s retreat toward Fort Jefferson.'

memes.label: "William Henry Harrison"
memes.description: 'American governor of the western territory and general whose 1811 march against the Prophet''s town produced the battle of Tippecanoe, and who later commanded on the north-western frontier in the War of 1812 that drew the Iroquois into the fighting of Red Jacket''s last years.'

memes.label: "William L. Stone"
memes.description: "American author and journalist whose 'Life and Times of Red Jacket' was the principal published biography of the Seneca orator prior to Hubbard's 1886 work."

memes.label: "William Savary"
memes.description: 'Quaker of the deputation present at the 1794 Canandaigua treaty whose Journal recorded Red Jacket''s oratory and family, later serving the account as a firsthand source.'

memes.label: "Winfield Scott"
memes.description: 'American brigade commander under General Brown on the Niagara frontier in 1814, whose disciplined brigade bore the brunt of the battle of Chippewa, in the campaign in which Red Jacket''s Seneca warriors fought as American allies.'

memes.label: "Witchcraft Accusations"
memes.description: "The Iroquois belief in witchcraft and sorcery — a system of supernatural explanation for misfortune, illness, and death that functioned as a mechanism of social control and, at times, political manipulation within Seneca society."

memes.label: "Young King"
memes.description: "Young King was a Seneca chief who led the Christian party within the Seneca Nation in the 1820s — the principal rival of Red Jacket and the leader of the faction that successfully deposed the old orator from his chieftainship in 1827."
